name: Anne's Cappuccino Squares
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 350F.
First Layer: Grease an 8-inch square cake pan with butter. Mix together thoroughly flour, confectioner's sugar, softened butter and coffee dissolved in hot water (use a food processor or electric mixer). Press mixture evenly into the pan bottom. Bake for 10 minutes in the preheated oven. Cool on a wire rack.
Second Layer: Meanwhile, in a large bowl, combine confectioner's sugar, 1 tablespoon coffee crystals dissolved in 2 tablespoons hot water, softened butter and 1 egg (lightly beaten with a fork at room temperature). Mix until thoroughly combined (use a food processor or electric mixer). Scald milk over medium heat until bubbles appear around the sides but it is not a rolling boil. Add the mixture from the bowl and heat for 7 minutes over medium heat, stirring frequently at first, then constantly after the mixture bubbles. If the mixture starts to stick on the bottom, lower the heat slightly. Let cool slightly, then pour over the first layer. Tilt the pan to cover the base layer evenly. Chill in the refrigerator for half an hour or more until set.
Third Layer: Combine 4 oz white chocolate with 1 tablespoon butter in a double boiler and melt (or melt 1-1 1/2 minutes in a microwave on High and stir). Spread over the chilled second layer with a spatula or blunt knife (the layer will be quite thin).
Optional Marbled Effect: Melt 2 oz semisweet chocolate in a double boiler (or in the microwave for 1-1 1/2 minutes on High and stir). Pour melted semisweet chocolate into a small ziploc bag and seal. Snip the corner of the bag with scissors. Squeeze out horizontal lines of semisweet chocolate onto the white chocolate layer. With a blunt knife, swirl the two chocolates together to create a marbled effect. Chill.
Before the third layer is completely set, score into bars.
Variation: Mocha Squares - substitute semisweet chocolate for white chocolate (this variation is easier as the middle layer and semisweet chocolate are similar in color).
Makes: approximately 32 bars (they are very sweet, so the bars should be small)
